anzsic_codes.name: Computer System Design and Related Services

merger_description:
Infosys Nova Holdings LLC proposes to acquire Optimum Achieve Holdings, Inc including Optimum’s subsidiary, Optimum Healthcare IT Pty Ltd.

Infosys Nova Holdings LLC is a wholly owned subsidiary of Infosys Limited (**Infosys**). Infosys is a global consulting and IT services company. Infosys provides a range of professional services across consulting, application development and maintenance, testing, business process management, cybersecurity, transformation, digital, cloud services, AI and automation, and data analytics. It is a leading provider of application sustainment, development and testing services. In Australia, Infosys provides services to Government and private sector organisations including in the financial services and telecommunications sectors.

Infosys is a foreign company registered in its original jurisdiction of India. Infosys trades as Infosys Technologies Limited in Australia.

Optimum Achieve Holdings, Inc is a digital transformation and consulting firm based in the US that specialises in providing services to the healthcare industry. Its broader suite of services includes digital transformation services, enterprise application services, managed services and application managed services, analytics and AI enablement, and cybersecurity and compliance.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Infosys Nova Holdings LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Infosys
Limited (Infosys), applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of Optimum Achieve Holdings, Inc (Optimum
Healthcare IT), as described in the transaction documents provided
as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Infosys is a global consulting and IT services company registered in
India. Infosys provides a range of professional services across
consulting, application development and maintenance, testing,
business process management, cybersecurity, transformation,
digital, cloud services, AI and automation, and data analytics. In
Australia, Infosys provides services to Government and private
sector organisations including in the financial services and
telecommunications sectors.
Optimum Healthcare IT is a digital transformation and consulting
firm based in the US that specialises in providing services to the
healthcare industry. Its broader suite of services includes digital
transformation services, enterprise application services, managed
services and application managed services, analytics and AI
enablement, and cybersecurity and compliance. In Australia,
Optimum Healthcare IT has limited operations.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Infosys and
Optimum Healthcare IT in the supply of IT implementation
and support services for healthcare services in Australia
b. Optimum Healthcare IT has a small presence in Australia
c. there are alternative suppliers of IT implementation and
support services for healthcare services in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
